zone_placement_rules.notes: shop.pattern.md explicitly permits this: 'Center the entrance ... unless a specific narrative or spatial reason requires an off-center door.' A citing MapIntent supplies that reason.

Pushes browse_loop into a side extension off the main rectangle, producing the L-shaped footprint this tier is named for. The room remains functionally one connected space (still satisfies PAT-INTERIOR-SHOP's 'single room' required condition -- an irregular single-room footprint, not a second walled-off room).

variants.notes: browse_loop alcove on the left of the main rectangle.
browse_loop alcove on the right of the main rectangle.

provenance.notes: The off-center door itself is directly permitted by PAT-INTERIOR-SHOP's own Layout Rules. The L-shaped alcove footprint is this contract's own extrapolation beyond the pattern's evidence -- neither observed shop sample (there is only one, Map021.json) has a non-rectangular shell. Filed at experimental confidence (below the source pattern's own medium ceiling) specifically because this geometric extrapolation, not the door offset, is unevidenced. Per WO-0059 task 6, this is the explicit record of a desired variation current evidence does not fully support -- treat as a candidate worth playtesting, not an accepted fact.
